operating_state: Net sales reached $41.4 billion in Q3 2025, increasing 2.8% driven primarily by the GMS acquisition contributing $892 million following its September 4 completion. Comparable sales increased 0.2% with average ticket growth of 1.8% offsetting a 1.6% decline in customer transactions, reflecting customer focus on smaller projects amid macroeconomic uncertainties and high interest rates pressuring large project demand. Online sales grew 11.4% to represent 15.2% of total sales.
cost_structure: Gross margin remained flat at 33.4% as lower shrink and supply chain benefits in the Primary segment offset the inclusion of GMS operations. SG&A expenses increased 5.9% to $7.6 billion, rising to 18.5% of sales from 17.9% due to higher payroll costs and GMS acquisition transaction expenses. Depreciation and amortization increased 3.9% to $826 million while maintaining 2.0% of sales.
material_events: Completed the GMS acquisition on September 4, 2025 for $5.5 billion in aggregate cash consideration, enhancing SRS's position as a multi-category building materials distributor. Announced a 2.2% quarterly dividend increase to $2.30 per share in February 2025. Opened three new U.S. stores during Q3, bringing total store count to 2,356.
macro_exposures: Continued monitoring of tariff and trade policy developments with ongoing supply chain diversification efforts and cost mitigation initiatives to maintain customer value proposition. High interest rate environment persisting and pressuring demand for large home improvement projects. Foreign exchange headwinds reduced net sales by $18 million in Q3 and $455 million year-to-date.
forward_outlook: Planning approximately $4 billion in capital expenditures for fiscal 2025, representing about 2.5% of net sales, focused on customer experience improvements, technology development, Pro capabilities expansion, and new store construction. Share repurchases remain paused through fiscal 2025 following the SRS acquisition pause initiated in March 2024. Management maintains disciplined capital allocation prioritizing business investment, dividend payments, then excess cash returns.
uncertainty_profile: Trade policy discussions remain ongoing with unpredictable ultimate business impact on future operations and cash flows. Inventory turnover declined to 4.5 times from 4.8 times due to higher average inventory levels during the nine-month period. ROIC decreased to 26.3% from 31.5% primarily due to higher average debt and equity from acquisition financing activities.
